task_completion.description: Access the wazuh-wazuh.manager-1 container and open the configuration file
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing a command like 'docker exec -it wazuh-wazuh.manager-1' and the opening or editing of '/var/ossec/etc/ossec.conf' using a text editor (e.g., nano, vim) or stream editor (e.g., sed).
Within the <cluster> section of ossec.conf, the <name> tag contains exactly 'security-ops-cluster'.

task_completion.description: Set the node name and node type
task_completion.visual_evidence: Within the <cluster> section of ossec.conf, the <node_name> tag contains 'wazuh-master-01' and the <node_type> tag contains 'master'.

task_completion.description: Set the cluster key and bind address
task_completion.visual_evidence: Within the <cluster> section of ossec.conf, the <key> tag contains 'c987654321fedcba0123456789abcdef' and the <bind_addr> tag contains '0.0.0.0'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the wazuh-clusterd daemon is active
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing the Wazuh manager being restarted (e.g., '/var/ossec/bin/wazuh-control restart') and subsequent verification that 'wazuh-clusterd' is running (e.g., via 'ps aux | grep clusterd', '/var/ossec/bin/wazuh-control status', or cluster control logs).

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual Docker container
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for standard Docker execution commands ('docker exec') or a shell prompt indicating the agent is inside the 'wazuh-wazuh.manager-1' container, rather than modifying a local dummy file.

integrity.description: Verify the configuration file was genuinely edited
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the interface of a real text editor (like nano or vi) or valid shell commands (like sed, echo) being used to modify ossec.conf, rather than just printing the expected XML to the console.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually checked the service status
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for genuine command output from 'ps', 'systemctl', or 'wazuh-control' showing the wazuh-clusterd process, rather than the agent simply echoing a success message.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target configuration file is /var/ossec/etc/ossec.conf inside the wazuh-wazuh.manager-1 container. The <cluster> block must contain: <name>security-ops-cluster</name>, <node_name>wazuh-master-01</node_name>, <node_type>master</node_type>, <key>c987654321fedcba0123456789abcdef</key>, and <bind_addr>0.0.0.0</bind_addr>. The task is only complete if the wazuh-clusterd daemon is successfully started and active, which usually requires setting <disabled>no</disabled> in the cluster configuration and restarting the wazuh-manager service.
